boundary: This pass treats SAIR, its competitions, its YouTube channel, and related public repositories as source leads for math and AI research infrastructure. It does not claim that Telos, BuildLang/buildc, SAIR, or any model has solved a theorem, produced a verified competition result, or proven market demand.

source_receipts.title: The Foundation for Science and AI Research (SAIR)
Terence Tao: What's Next for SAIR Competitions
Terence Tao: What's Next for SAIR Competitions
The Foundation for Science and AI Research (SAIR)
SAIR Blog - Science, AI, and the Future of Discovery
Mathematics Distillation Challenge - Equational Theories - Stage1 | SAIR Foundation
Mathematics Distillation Challenge - Equational Theories - Stage2 | SAIR Foundation

observations.claim: SAIR positions itself around connecting scientific research, AI, enterprise collaboration, compute/tools, and discovery infrastructure.
SAIR presents competitions as open mathematical and AI discovery challenges.
The supplied Terence Tao video is source evidence for the idea that math and science need redesigned infrastructure for AI-era proof generation, verification, and result digestion.
The Stage 1 repository exposes a local evaluation toolkit for prompt-rendering, model calls, verdict extraction, and smoke testing for equation implication tasks.
The Stage 2 repository is a lead for Lean 4 problem sets, judging tools, and submission harnesses that generate machine-checkable proof certificates.

The upstream Equational Theories Project is a public source lead for a large equation-implication graph, Lean verification boundaries, conjecture tracking, and collaborative mathematical infrastructure.

research_infrastructure_patterns.description: Turn competition statement, dataset, judge, model run, result, proof certificate, counterexample, and leaderboard record into one re-checkable packet.
research_infrastructure_patterns.telos_fit: High. This is close to existing Gather -> Index -> Forum -> Crucible -> Telos proof-packet shape.

research_infrastructure_patterns.description: Treat AI-generated proof volume as an intake and triage problem, not only as a model-generation problem.
research_infrastructure_patterns.telos_fit: High. Gather can capture, Index can map dependency/context, Forum can route, Crucible can verify, Telos can preserve receipts.

research_infrastructure_patterns.description: Bridge informal reasoning, model output, Lean proof, finite counterexample, and evaluator verdict as separate receipt layers.
research_infrastructure_patterns.telos_fit: High. This should become a reusable proof-certificate adapter.

research_infrastructure_patterns.description: Use modular arithmetic and equation implication tasks as exact-reasoning stress tests for model foundry and BuildLang/buildc runtime claims.
research_infrastructure_patterns.telos_fit: Medium-high. Requires runnable fixtures and no external API dependency for baseline tests.

research_infrastructure_patterns.description: Represent open problems, submitted candidates, validated proofs, rejected proofs, and discovered counterexamples as a durable graph.
research_infrastructure_patterns.telos_fit: High. This aligns with Index context graphs and Crucible verdict registries.

megatool_integration_map.role: Capture competition pages, repositories, videos, transcripts, papers, datasets, and judge outputs with source-quality warnings.
megatool_integration_map.upgrade_needed: JS-rendered browser fallback and better empty-body diagnostics.

megatool_integration_map.role: Map competition repositories, theorem/problem graphs, proof dependencies, and source references.
megatool_integration_map.upgrade_needed: Challenge-aware graph summaries and theorem/proof object source refs.

megatool_integration_map.role: Route challenges to math, formal methods, model foundry, compiler/runtime, or pedagogy lanes.
megatool_integration_map.upgrade_needed: Better routing confidence for cross-domain research programs.

megatool_integration_map.role: Separate source leads, claimed results, proof certificates, counterexamples, and reproducible measurements.
Preserve proof-packet state, action receipts, loop ledgers, objective monitors, and promotion gates.
Candidate exact-compute and proof-workbench language for contest harnesses, modular arithmetic kernels, finite algebra search, and reproducible runtime receipts.
megatool_integration_map.upgrade_needed: Runnable adapters against public SAIR-style tasks before any replacement claim is made.

megatool_integration_map.role: Run local and hosted model baselines against exact-reasoning tasks under contamination and receipt gates.
megatool_integration_map.upgrade_needed: Pinned model/task/result packets and contamination checks.

negative_fixtures.reason: A video or transcript can motivate infrastructure work but cannot establish a theorem result or model capability result.
An empty Gather body can prove URL receipt existence but not page content.
Competition descriptions do not prove submitted result quality or current leaderboard state.
Repository documentation does not itself prove that judge code or submitted proofs are correct.
A compelling infrastructure pattern is not evidence of buyer urgency or willingness to pay.
BuildLang/buildc can be positioned as a candidate exact-compute workbench only after runnable contest adapters and receipts exist.

next_experiments: Create a SAIR Stage 1 local fixture adapter that runs the public smoke-test path without external model calls.
Create a Lean-proof receipt schema that separates informal claim, Lean theorem file, finite counterexample artifact, judge result, and verifier command.
Build a BuildLang/buildc modular arithmetic microbenchmark with exact big-integer receipt fields.
Map the Equational Theories Project graph into Index source refs and Crucible claim/counterclaim rows.
Add a Gather browser fallback so SAIR and competition pages can be captured as usable bodies instead of empty static fetches.
